Curated Selections

Our Menu

Everything on our menu is made with care. Fresh ingredients, real recipes, and a little bit of personality in every dish.

Midnight Web Bruschetta $14

Toasted sourdough topped with roasted tomatoes, whipped ricotta, fresh basil, and a little drizzle of truffle honey. Simple but really good.

Chef's Pick
Blue Tide Oysters $22

Six East Coast oysters served with a champagne mignonette and pickled cucumber on the side. Fresh and clean, the way oysters should be.

Silk Thread Calamari $17

Fried calamari rings, crispy outside, tender inside. Comes with a smoky aioli and a little herb salad to keep things light.

Dark Web Soup $12

Squid ink soup with blue crab and a swirl of crème fraîche on top. It sounds fancy but it's honestly just a really comforting bowl.

Vegan Option
Arachnid Filet Mignon $54

An 8oz filet, cooked however you like it. Comes with truffle butter, roasted bone marrow on the side, wilted spinach, and a peppercorn sauce.

Signature
Cobweb Linguine Nero $32

Squid ink pasta with seared scallops, cherry tomatoes, and our own blue crab sauce. One of the most popular things on the menu for good reason.

Midnight Duck Confit $38

Slow-cooked duck leg, really tender, with a cherry and port sauce, celery root purée, and crispy lentils for some crunch.

The Weaver Salmon $36

Pan-seared salmon over blue corn grits with a charred leek cream, a fresh cucumber salsa, and lemon oil. Light but filling.

Shadow Mushroom Risotto $28

Creamy arborio rice with a mix of wild mushrooms, mascarpone, parmesan, and black garlic oil stirred through. Great for vegetarians or anyone who just loves risotto.

Vegetarian
The Black Widow Cake $14

Dark chocolate cake with an espresso kick, blackberry sauce, and mascarpone cream. Rich without being too heavy. A lot of people order two.

Must Try
Blue Velvet Panna Cotta $11

A butterfly pea panna cotta that turns this beautiful deep blue. Topped with elderflower jelly, blueberry compote, and a little candied lavender.

Web Spun Crème Brûlée $12

Classic vanilla custard with a perfectly crackly sugar top. Served with fresh raspberries and mint. Sometimes the classics are just the best.

The Spider's Venom $18

Blue Curaçao, vodka, elderflower liqueur, and lime with a tonic top. Comes out this electric blue color , looks cool, tastes even better.

House Special
Midnight Margarita $16

Añejo tequila, blood orange, Cointreau, and an activated charcoal syrup that makes it run dark. Smoked salt rim. Strong but smooth.

Cobalt Mocktail $9

Butterfly pea tea, yuzu, ginger beer, muddled blueberries, and fresh mint. No alcohol, but honestly you won't miss it. Really refreshing.

Aranea Old Fashioned $19

Bourbon, charcoal bitters, smoked maple, and an orange peel over a big black ice sphere. If you like whiskey drinks, this one's for you.

A Visual Feast

Gallery

Take a look around. Our space and our food both have a certain vibe , dark, cozy, and always delicious.

2005 Est. NYC
Our Story

A Little Spot
We Call Home

Spider's Place was created by Hunter College student Sammi Mushtaq , someone who grew up loving food and always dreamed of having a place to share that with people.

It started simple. Sammi spent a lot of late nights in the kitchen experimenting with recipes between study sessions, cooking for friends who never had anything bad to say. Eventually those dinners turned into something bigger. The idea for Spider's Place came out of wanting to create a restaurant that felt different , not stuffy, not trying too hard, just genuinely good food in a space that actually has some personality to it.

We picked the Upper East Side because it felt right. The neighborhood has energy, and we wanted Spider's Place to fit into that. The dining room is dim and cozy with deep blue lighting throughout , the kind of place you actually want to stay a while.

Every dish on our menu came from real testing and tasting. Nothing is on there just to look good. If it didn't taste right, it didn't make the cut. That's still how we do things.

5K+ Guests Served
40+ Menu Items
